name: Arc Tanks Deluxe
description: A Pocket Tanks-inspired two-player score artillery duel with 50 carefully balanced weapons, a 20-weapon draft, 10 weapons and 10 shots per side, persistent aim, finite movement, full-field material terrain, circular craters, dirt building, tunnels, liquids, bouncy terrain, and lasting fire scoring.
rules:
OVERVIEW
Two tanks duel on a full-screen generated battlefield in score mode. The 50-weapon curated catalog is unlocked. Each match opens in the Weapon Shop with 20 random weapons. Player 1 may choose Random All to immediately deal 10 random weapons to each side, or Player 1 and Player 2 can alternate picks until each side has 10 weapons. Battle lasts 20 total shots, 10 per side, or until both drafted loadouts are exhausted. Higher score wins; ties draw.

VISIBLE STATE
The public view shows phase, draft pool, both loadouts, used weapons, saved aim, finite movement fuel, score, wind, turn, tanks, active fire/lava hazards, a 2D material terrain grid, the complete weapon catalog, and last-shot timing/impact/terrain-operation/burn data for animation. Terrain material intervals include dirt, bouncy, glue, concrete, water, lava, and scorch. Weapons permanently cut circular craters, carve tunnels, shave terrain, build dirt and walls, paint bouncy/glue/concrete terrain, pour liquid, extinguish hazards with water or dirt, and leave short fire/lava hazards.

ACTIONS
During weapon select Player 1 may submit {"type":"draft_random_all"} before any picks, or either player may submit {"type":"draft_pick","slot":0,"weapon":"single_shot"} for an unclaimed draft slot on their pick. During battle submit {"type":"fire","weapon":"single_shot","move":0,"angle":45,"power":82,"note":"brief plan"}. Move is a signed pre-shot tank movement in world units, capped each turn and by remaining move fuel. Angle is 5 to 85 degrees upward toward the enemy. Power is 20 to 100. Each player's angle and power persist until changed. There is no projected aiming line or impact preview.

WEAPONS
Every weapon has an archetype with concrete terrain behavior: blast, big crater, chain reaction, firecracker, splitter, dirtball, cruball, dirt slinger, wall, earth mover, crater maker, digger, drillers, bunker buster, tunnel, laser, torpedo, roller, skipper, homing, rain, carpet, liquid, fire, flamethrower, lava, and mower. Fire, lava, napalm, and flamethrower-style weapons last two turns and score by ember exposure: concentrated fire in a pit scores heavily, while fire on flat ground spreads out and scores lightly. Dirtball-style weapons bury and pin tanks when they land close, while water and dirt can put out existing flames.

SCORING
Enemy hits add points to the shooter. Self-hits subtract points. Burn hazards score by exposure over multiple ticks. Terrain-only weapons may score little or nothing but can build cover, bury tanks, expose tanks, extinguish hazards, or set up future shots.
